dialogues.text: Dear, I'm a bit short for my medication this week. Could you spare $4?
dialogues.yes_response: God bless you. You remind me of my grandson.

dialogues.text: My groceries are heavy. Could you carry them to the corner for me? I have $5 for you.
dialogues.yes_response: Oh thank you sweetheart. Right this way.
dialogues.no_response: I'll take it slow. I always do.

dialogues.text: The heating oil company needs $6 by Friday or they'll cut me off.
dialogues.yes_response: I'll light a candle for you tonight.
dialogues.no_response: I'll call my daughter. She'll understand.

dialogues.text: Could you help me reach that box on the top shelf? My back isn't what it used to be. $3.
dialogues.yes_response: Oh, just the blue one please. Thank you dear.
dialogues.no_response: I'll wait for my neighbor then.

dialogues.text: I'm baking bread for the church sale. Could you deliver the loaves for me? $7.
dialogues.yes_response: Ask for Sister Agnes. She'll take them.
dialogues.no_response: I suppose I'll take the bus.

dialogues.text: Lost $2 and I need exact change for the bus. Would you happen to have it?
dialogues.no_response: I'll ask the driver to be kind.

dialogues.text: Could you read this letter for me? My glasses broke. I'll give you $3.
dialogues.yes_response: Go ahead dear. Read slowly please.
dialogues.no_response: My granddaughter will read it tomorrow.

dialogues.text: I'm short $8 for the phone bill. They'll disconnect me.
dialogues.yes_response: You're so kind. The world needs more people like you.
dialogues.no_response: I'll pray on it. Something will come.

dialogues.text: Water my garden while I'm at the clinic? $5 and you can take some tomatoes.
dialogues.yes_response: The hose is by the back gate. Thank you dear.
dialogues.no_response: They might wilt but they're tough little things.

dialogues.text: I'm $3 short for the candles at the Sunday service.
dialogues.yes_response: I'll say a little prayer for you.
dialogues.no_response: We'll use fewer candles. God will understand.
